title: Tanker report - Week 6
summary:
VLCC
The market in the Middle East dropped further this week, with the deepening impact of the coronavirus outbreak on oil demand from China keeping activity low. Rates for 270,000mt to China fell 12.5 points to the low WS 40s, while 280,000mt to US Gulf (USG) via the Capes came down almost 15 per cent to WS 30. In the Atlantic, regional rates for 260,000mt West Africa to China reduced by 10 points, to WS 47.5 region.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The market in the Middle East dropped further this week, with the deepening impact of the coronavirus outbreak on oil demand from China keeping activity low. Rates for 270,000mt to China fell 12.5 points to the low WS 40s, while 280,000mt to US Gulf (USG) via the Capes came down almost 15 per cent to WS 30.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Rates for 130,000 West Africa to UK-Continent (UKC) weakened to WS 81. However, they bounced back to WS 87.5/90 level on Thursday, representing a drop of about 10 points week-on-week.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: Rates for 80,000mt Ceyhan to Mediterranean voyages continued their downhill quest for the bottom, losing a further 19 points, to WS 72.5 level. In Northern Europe, rates for 80,000mt Cross-North Sea lost another 22 points this week and came to WS 95 level.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: It was an uneventful week for owners, with the market in the Middle East Gulf to Japan trade for 75,000mt remaining steady in the very low WS 80s. In the 55,000mt size, the market settled in low WS 90s.
